ab: ana cu mosa mita isu?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: uwah, m’es lungan mu.
ch: B:可以來，我很願意。
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: ana cu mosa mita isu?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: laxiy uwah, ini piyux ba’un mu.
ch: B:不要來，我知道的不多。
en: B: Don't come, I don't know much.

ab: ungat yaw su ’lyan? ana cu mosa mita isu?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: ana, n_gon misu sali.
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: ungat yaw su cuxan? aki cu mosa tkba isu yaw na lela na Tayal.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於泰雅族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary stories of the Atayal people.

ab: mosa cu rgyax cuxan, iyat cu kaki sali.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: pnungan mu ga piyux ci ba’un su knxan gaga lela na Tayal, smoya cu mosa tkba isu.
ch: A:我聽說您對泰雅族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very knowledgeable about the traditional customs of the Atayal and I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: ini piyux knba’an mu, ana ga mosa misu sba’an kwara knba’an mu.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: ana misu galun lutux? maha kya ga mosa ba’un sbiru babaw nya.
ch: A:我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: ana, cikuy na yaw.
ch: B:好的，沒問題。
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: pisa kawas su micu la?
ch: A:您今年幾歲了?
en: A:How old are you?

ab: waya cu rima mhenas m’esu pgan spat kawas la.
ch: B:我已經98歲了。
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: mrkyas su ci.
ch: A:您真年輕。
en: A: You're so young.

ab: henu la! nyu cu rima bkis la.
ch: B:沒有啦!我已經老了。
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: kahun inu knholan su?
ch: A:您故鄉來自哪裡?
en: A:Where is your hometown from?

ab: kahun cu llyung Mklesan alang na Pyahaw.
ch: B:我來自葛蕾扇流域比亞毫部落。
en: B: I'm from the Bihue tribe of the Greyfriars.

ab: Tayal nerin su uyi?
ch: A:您的太太也是泰雅族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also an Atayal?

ab: iyat, heya ga Truku.
ch: B:不，她是太魯閣族人。
en: B: No, she's a Taroko.

ab: pisa kwara ke ba’un su kmayan?
ch: A:您會說哪些語言?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: kba cu kmayan ke na Tayal ru ke na Truku.
ch: B: 我會說泰雅語和太魯閣語。
en: B: I speak Atayal and Telugu.

ab: p’asun ci thoyay cu mwah mita tkba isu, ana su kmayan cikah gaga na mluyak?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ana, mrkyas cu ga mosa cu mluyak rgyax kryax.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: mhway su, talagay myasa ci pnsba su soni.
ch: A:謝謝您，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: knayan misu ga calay ci ini kbrus kwara, kahun pnsba na bnkisan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: tehok cahay tkba misu kisa. mhway su ci kwara pnsba su.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: iya cayux, tisan ta loyi.
ch: B:不客氣，我們再聊。
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: mosa cu la, babaw nya ga smoya cu mung piyux hayi yaw na lela.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: myasa! thway mosa.
ch: B:好喔!請慢走。
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
